items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.description: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
items.titleIt: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky

items.title: Ethiopia: Ethiopia Consolidates Continental Leadership in Electric Vehicle Infrastructure
items.description: [ENA] Addis Ababa -- Ethiopia is strengthening its position as Africa's leading adopter of electric vehicles by expanding reliable and resilient charging infrastructure across the country, State Minister of Transport and Logistics Bareo Hassen said.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: Ethiopia Consolidates Continental Leadership in Electric Vehicle Infrastructure

items.title: Africa: Mombasa Declaration Unites 15 Countries in Fight Against Illegal Fishing
items.description: [allAfrica] Mombasa, Kenya -- As illegal, unreported and unregulated (IUU) fishing practices continue to deplete fish populations as well as contribute to human rights abuses at sea, the demand for transparency within the global fishing industry is growing. An estimated one in five fish consumed globally is linked to illegal fishing, a practice often associated with forced labour, human trafficking and environmental destruction. It is estimated that more than 120,000 fishers are currently trapped in situations of modern slavery
items.titleIt: Africa: Mombasa Declaration Unites 15 Countries in Fight Against Illegal Fishing

items.title: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi had assured him of the safe departure of an Italian ship, the Grimaldi Group's car carrier Grande Torino, that has been blocked in the Persian Gulf for months. Araghchi, said Tajani on X, reporting on a phone call with his counterpart, "assured me that in the next few hours the Iranian authorities will ensure the conditions for the safe departure of the Italian Grimaldi ship 'Grande Torino,'" "blocked for months in the Gulf." (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship

items.title: G7 Turns spotlight on Middle East as Trump unveils Iran deal
items.description: G7 leaders and invited partner nations gathered in Evian-les-Bains as attention shifted from Ukraine to the Middle East following Donald Trump’s announcement of a tentative deal with Iran, with Gulf leaders joining talks on regional security and global economic risks.
items.titleIt: G7 Turns spotlight on Middle East as Trump unveils Iran deal

items.title: Sudan: MSF Sacks 18 Staff in Eastern Chad for Sexual Exploitation of Sudanese Women and Underage Girls
items.description: [Dabanga] El Tina, Eastern Chad / Amsterdam -- The medical NGO Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) has confirmed that 18 members of its staff in eastern Chad have been dismissed for serious misconduct, "and are now barred from working with MSF", after "serious allegations of sexual exploitation and abuse were reported by Sudanese refugee women". Responding to questions from Radio Dabanga yesterday, MSF press liaison acknowledged that in late 2024, serious allegations of sexual exploitation and abuse were reported by Sudanese refugee women
items.titleIt: Sudan: MSF Sacks 18 Staff in Eastern Chad for Sexual Exploitation of Sudanese Women and Underage Girls
